rankings.title: Does 340B Drug Pricing Crowd Out Medicaid Patients? Cross-Payer RDD Evidence from Provider-Level Claims
conditions: convincing no-manipulation case after donuts
demonstrating a strong first-stage—eligibility actually changes 340B participation
documenting T‑MSIS state-quality screens don’t drive results

rankings.title: Cross-Payer Spillovers at Medicare Payment Boundaries: Do Medicare Rate Cuts Starve the Medicaid Safety Net?
Does Federal “Underserved” Designation Actually Attract Medicaid Home Care Providers? RDD at the MUA Threshold
conditions: verifying that observed IMU at decision time predicts designation with a strong discontinuity
restricting to county MUAs where IMU is well-measured
showing continuity of pre-trends in HCBS supply proxies

rankings.title: The Hidden Cost of Hospital Consolidation: 340B Eligibility and the Vertical Integration of Medicaid Drug Services
conditions: validating the integration measure against an external ownership/affiliation dataset
pre-specifying an access/spending consequence beyond organizational shares

rankings.title: Nurse Practitioner Full Practice Authority and the Medicaid HCBS Workforce: A Border Discontinuity Design
Idea 1: Does 340B Drug Pricing Crowd Out Medicaid Patients? Cross-Payer RDD
conditions: passing McCrary density tests with the donut specification
demonstrating a clear first stage in 340B participation

rankings.title: Idea 4: Cross-Payer Spillovers at Medicare Payment Boundaries
conditions: verifying that GPCI payment differences at the chosen borders are economically large enough to plausibly shift provider behavior
